content: Hi, I'm Rowan, and I'm a Social Worker specializing in foster care. I've been working on a project to improve the foster care system in Raleigh, NC.
Hi Rowan, that's a wonderful project! What inspired you to focus on foster care, and what specific areas of the system are you trying to improve?

I've always been passionate about helping children, and I've seen firsthand the impact that a stable and loving home can have on a child's life. I'm working with a team to develop a new training program for foster parents, which will launch on March 1st.
That sounds like a valuable resource. What kind of training will the program provide, and how will it support foster parents in their roles?

The program will cover topics like child development, trauma-informed care, and cultural competency. We're also partnering with local organizations to provide ongoing support and resources for foster families.

It's great that you're taking a holistic approach to supporting foster families. How do you balance your work with taking care of your aging parents, and are there any challenges you're facing in that regard?

It can be tough, but I'm lucky to have a supportive team at work and a strong network of family and friends. My parents are actually moving in with me next month, so I'm preparing for that transition.

That's a big change, but it's wonderful that you're able to support your parents in this way. Have you thought about how you'll manage your work and personal life during this time, and are there any resources you're using to help with caregiving?
I'm trying to prioritize my time and set clear boundaries, but it's not always easy. I've been looking into local resources for caregivers, and I've also been practicing piano to help me relax.
Piano is a great way to unwind! Do you have a favorite piece to play, or are you working on any new compositions?
I love playing Chopin's nocturnes, they're so soothing. I've also been thinking about starting a travel blog to document my adventures and share my experiences with others.
A travel blog sounds like a fantastic idea! Have you thought about where you'd like to travel to next, and how you'll balance your love of travel with your work and caregiving responsibilities?

I'd love to visit Japan and try all the amazing food, but for now, I'm focusing on local trips and exploring the Raleigh area. I've been trying to prioritize my time and make the most of my free time.

Japan is definitely a food lover's paradise! Don't forget to take care of yourself and prioritize your own needs, especially during this busy time. Are there any other hobbies or interests you're passionate about, aside from piano and travel?
